primary_thesis_sentence: ORIUS provides a reliability-aware runtime safety layer for physical AI under degraded observation, enforcing certificate-backed action release through uncertainty coverage, repair, and fallback.
main_theory_claim: Observation-only mandatory-release controllers face a lower bound under safety-relevant ambiguity; ORIUS achieves an alpha-bounded upper guarantee under covered uncertainty sets.
main_empirical_claim: The promoted runtime-denominator rows show zero ORIUS TSVR for Battery and Healthcare and epsilon-closed ORIUS TSVR for Autonomous Vehicles under bounded predeployment validation.

source_anchors.source_name: NASA Formal Verification Framework for Runtime Assurance
source_anchors.supports: Runtime assurance motivates a trusted runtime layer that intervenes before unsafe release.
source_anchors.orius_gap: ORIUS adds observation-reliability, certificate-backed release, and cross-domain artifact discipline.
source_anchors.boundary: Positioning source only; not evidence of ORIUS deployment certification.

source_anchors.supports: Coverage-aware uncertainty gives the statistical vocabulary for alpha-bounded release claims.
source_anchors.orius_gap: ORIUS connects coverage sets to runtime action release and fail-closed certificates.
source_anchors.boundary: Does not create a new universal conformal theorem by itself.

source_anchors.source_name: Control Barrier Functions: Theory and Applications
source_anchors.supports: Safety-critical control literature motivates set-based action admissibility and invariance.
source_anchors.orius_gap: ORIUS focuses on observation ambiguity and reliability-aware release rather than only nominal-state safety.
source_anchors.boundary: ORIUS is not claimed to replace all barrier-function controllers.

source_anchors.supports: Retrospective ICU/EHR evidence source for healthcare monitoring validation.
source_anchors.orius_gap: ORIUS evaluates runtime alert/fallback legality and calibration rather than only point prediction.
source_anchors.boundary: Retrospective evidence only; not live clinical deployment.

source_anchors.supports: Multi-center ICU data source for stronger source/site-holdout validation.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Adds a path to external source validation beyond single-source retrospective replay.
source_anchors.boundary: Access-controlled dataset; future validation tier unless artifacts exist.

source_anchors.supports: Reporting discipline for prediction-model development and validation with AI/ML.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Forces clear calibration, validation, population, and utility reporting for healthcare ORIUS.
source_anchors.boundary: Reporting standard, not proof of clinical effectiveness.

source_anchors.supports: Risk-of-bias and applicability checks for clinical prediction models.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Defines reviewer-safe subgroup, source-holdout, and applicability claims.
source_anchors.boundary: Does not authorize live clinical use.

source_anchors.supports: Clinical-trial reporting expectations for AI interventions.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Clarifies that ORIUS healthcare remains retrospective and is not prospective trial evidence.
source_anchors.boundary: No prospective clinical trial is claimed.

source_anchors.supports: Early live clinical evaluation expectations for AI decision support.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Supplies the boundary between retrospective monitoring evidence and live decision-support evidence.
source_anchors.boundary: ORIUS does not claim DECIDE-AI live evaluation completion.

source_anchors.supports: Diagnostic AI reporting expectations and external evaluation caution.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Helps prevent healthcare ORIUS from overstating diagnostic or clinical-decision status.
source_anchors.boundary: Healthcare row is monitoring/runtime evidence, not diagnostic approval.

source_anchors.supports: Large-scale closed-loop planning benchmark with city-diverse real driving data.
source_anchors.orius_gap: ORIUS uses nuPlan replay/surrogate evidence now and treats full closed-loop validation as a next tier.
source_anchors.boundary: Current completed evidence is bounded replay/surrogate unless closed-loop artifacts exist.

source_anchors.supports: Controllable simulator for weather, sensors, traffic, latency, and emergency stress tests.
source_anchors.orius_gap: CARLA is the best next tier for repeatable degraded-observation closed-loop stress.
source_anchors.boundary: CARLA completion cannot be claimed until a real CARLA runtime loop produces artifacts.

source_anchors.supports: Motion prediction/replay data with tracks, map context, and train/validation/test splits.
source_anchors.orius_gap: Supports AV runtime replay evidence but not closed-loop road deployment.
source_anchors.boundary: Dataset replay is not road deployment.

uplift_scorecard.implemented_lift: Source-backed novelty separation across runtime assurance, coverage-aware uncertainty, safety-critical control, clinical validation, AV simulation, and battery HIL tiers.
uplift_scorecard.remaining_blocker: Need final paper text to keep ORIUS framed as a unified observation-reliability release contract, not a predictor/filter/fallback-only system.
uplift_scorecard.acceptance_gate: Reviewer can identify the exact gap ORIUS closes relative to each source lane.

uplift_scorecard.implemented_lift: T4/T9/T10/T11 bridge is represented as necessity plus Bayes lower bound plus covered alpha upper bound with mechanized kernels, empirical T9/T10 discharge artifacts, and executable observation-ambiguity witnesses.
uplift_scorecard.remaining_blocker: Keep T9/T10 assumption-qualified and prevent any global-optimality or all-ambiguity-implies-violation overclaim beyond the discharged Battery/AV/Healthcare boundary-law artifacts.
uplift_scorecard.acceptance_gate: T4, T11, and T10_T11_ObservationAmbiguitySandwich stay synchronized across audit, registry, code, tests, paper, and appendix.

uplift_scorecard.implemented_lift: Runtime-denominator TSVR, fallback/intervention, calibration, witness rows, and fail-safe-normalized utility dominance are source-locked per domain.
uplift_scorecard.remaining_blocker: Keep AV and Healthcare utility-safety frontiers explicit in the paper so conservative fallback is reported as a measured cost, not hidden utility success.
uplift_scorecard.acceptance_gate: All promoted rows pass strict runtime gates and fail-safe-normalized utility/fallback tradeoffs are explicit.

uplift_scorecard.implemented_lift: Current package separates bounded nuPlan closed-loop planner evidence, retrospective healthcare source/site holdout, and battery software-HIL evidence from stronger future tiers.
uplift_scorecard.remaining_blocker: Complete prospective/live healthcare validation and physical or high-fidelity battery HIL; AV remains bounded nuPlan closed-loop rather than road deployment or CARLA.
uplift_scorecard.acceptance_gate: No paper claim uses next-tier validation language before the corresponding artifact exists.

uplift_scorecard.implemented_lift: Package marks max freeze incomplete until release manifest and hash locks exist.
uplift_scorecard.remaining_blocker: Finish max freeze, remove AppleDouble/git hygiene noise, run clean full pytest with mutation guard, and publish frozen hashes.
uplift_scorecard.acceptance_gate: predeployment_release_manifest.json plus frozen hash CSV/JSON exist and validators pass.

uplift_scorecard.implemented_lift: All source-backed language is bounded predeployment validation and explicitly rejects road, clinical, and unrestricted field deployment claims.
uplift_scorecard.remaining_blocker: Keep manuscript, README, claim ledger, and package synchronized after every new validation run.
uplift_scorecard.acceptance_gate: Claim validators reject deployment overclaims and validation-harness headline evidence.
